title: The One With All The Kissing

jokes.text: You look cute in bubbles. / You're just all liquored up.
jokes.setup: Sweet compliment about looking cute in bubbles
jokes.punchline: Dismissive response attributing the compliment to alcohol
jokes.explanation: Simple deflection joke with good economy but predictable structure

jokes.text: I've had a very long, hard day. / Want some chicken?
jokes.setup: Someone expressing they've had a difficult day
jokes.punchline: Joey's completely tone-deaf offer of chicken
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey character beat - his obliviousness to emotional context

jokes.text: You sure? Some extra-crispy, dirty rice? / For the last time, no! Get out, Joey!
jokes.setup: Joey persisting despite clear rejection
jokes.punchline: The specificity of 'extra-crispy, dirty rice' and explosive rejection
jokes.explanation: Joey's food obsession escalating beyond social awareness

jokes.text: He kept asking me if I wanted chicken. / Chicken? / I could eat some chicken.
jokes.setup: Complaint about Joey's persistent chicken offers
jokes.punchline: Immediate interest in chicken from the listener
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic reversal - the mention of chicken immediately creates appetite

jokes.text: Could I get a three-piece, some coleslaw, some beans and a Coke? / Diet Coke.
jokes.setup: Elaborate fast food order with multiple sides
jokes.punchline: Requesting diet soda with the unhealthy meal
jokes.explanation: Classic but overused diet soda with junk food observation

jokes.text: Was his name Angus? / What? / He was a cabdriver we had in London.
jokes.setup: Phoebe trying to tell a story about a cabdriver
jokes.punchline: Immediate derailment by London trip reminiscing
jokes.explanation: Sets up the running gag of London trip derailing conversations

jokes.text: The Wheat Sheaf! / And they had that beer... / Boddington's!
jokes.setup: Continuing London reminiscing
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific and enthusiastic memory sharing
jokes.explanation: Building the cringe of excluding Phoebe from shared memories

jokes.text: Pheebs was going to tell a story. / So, he had a really funny hat. / I don't want to talk about it.
jokes.setup: Someone trying to redirect back to Phoebe's story
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's deflated, minimal story and withdrawal
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures the deflation of being excluded and giving up on participation

jokes.text: Hey Ross! / Boddington's! / Oh, man! / I'd walk back to London for another frosty one of those bad boys.
jokes.setup: Ross arriving
jokes.punchline: Immediate London beer enthusiasm and hyperbolic declaration
jokes.explanation: Continues the London obsession running gag with typical guy hyperbole

jokes.text: They have them at the British pub by the Trade Center.
jokes.setup: Ross's dramatic declaration about walking to London for beer
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact revelation that it's available locally
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Ross's romantic nostalgia with practical reality

jokes.text: You abandoned her on a plane to Greece. / I did not 'abandon' Rachel. Okay? Emily showed up at the airport. I had to go after her. I did what I had to do. She's my wife. Rachel is my wife. / Emily! / Emily is my wife.
jokes.setup: Ross defending his actions in London
jokes.punchline: Freudian slip saying Rachel is his wife, then correction
jokes.explanation: Classic Freudian slip revealing Ross's true feelings, perfectly character-driven

jokes.text: I can't believe she can outrun you. / Hey! She's fast, okay? You think you can beat me? Let's go! Outside!
jokes.setup: Comment about Emily outrunning Ross
jokes.punchline: Ross's defensive challenge to a race to prove his speed
jokes.explanation: Ross's defensive masculinity manifesting in absurd physical challenge

jokes.text: Terrible? Hell, I was in Greece! There was a nice hotel, nice beach, met the nice people.
jokes.setup: Expectation that Greece trip was terrible
jokes.punchline: Rachel's enthusiastic description of how great it was
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection playing against expectation of misery

jokes.text: One for each day I've known and loved her.
jokes.setup: Ross sending 72 roses to Emily's parents
jokes.punchline: The overly romantic and mathematically precise reasoning
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross romantic obsessiveness with cringe-worthy specificity

jokes.text: When you make out the card, be sure to make it out to 'Emily'.
jokes.setup: Ross's elaborate romantic gesture
jokes.punchline: Dry advice referencing his wedding name mixup
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback to the wedding disaster with perfect economy and sting

jokes.text: People came up to me going, 'Mrs. Geller! Why are you cry?'
jokes.setup: Rachel revealing her actual miserable experience in Greece
jokes.punchline: The broken English of concerned Greeks addressing her by married name
jokes.explanation: Good specificity in the broken English and the irony of the wrong married name

jokes.text: London is stupid! Stupid!
jokes.setup: Rachel's emotional breakdown about bad decisions
jokes.punchline: Childish repeated insult of an entire city
jokes.explanation: Rachel's emotional regression to childish name-calling is character-appropriate

jokes.text: Oh, no. I did that for someone once. I'm not comfortable having that power over someone's life.
jokes.setup: Rachel asking Phoebe to make all her decisions
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's ominous refusal with dark implications
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe mystique - suggesting dark past experiences with casual delivery

jokes.text: I'll do it.
jokes.setup: Phoebe refusing to make Rachel's decisions due to past trauma
jokes.punchline: Monica immediately volunteering, revealing her controlling nature
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica character beat - can't resist taking control even with warning

jokes.text: Honey, sweetie, by the edges. / Edges! Fingers! Smudgy!
jokes.setup: Monica trying to protect photos while Rachel handles them emotionally
jokes.punchline: Monica's increasing panic about photo damage
jokes.explanation: Classic Monica obsessiveness with escalating panic over minor details

jokes.text: That's okay. I know that you're very upset right now. I know that wasn't about me. / I bet it was about her a little.
jokes.setup: Monica being understanding about Rachel's emotional outburst
jokes.punchline: Dry observation that it was probably partially about Monica
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan acknowledgment of the obvious truth Monica wants to ignore

jokes.text: Look. Gunther.
jokes.setup: Phoebe pointing out available guys for Rachel
jokes.punchline: Gunther as an example of a 'great guy'
jokes.explanation: Running gag about Gunther's unrequited love being obvious to everyone

jokes.text: Say that to him, and you're golden.
jokes.setup: Rachel saying she has to do what Monica tells her
jokes.punchline: Monica's advice to literally tell the guy that line
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion - Monica's terrible advice to use submissiveness as pickup line

jokes.text: And me. / We've been talking about London too much. / Sorry, it's just because I couldn't be there.
jokes.setup: Photo description that clearly excludes Phoebe
jokes.punchline: Awkward acknowledgment and Phoebe's sad explanation
jokes.explanation: Painful continuation of Phoebe exclusion theme with genuine pathos

jokes.text: All I get to do now is pregnant stuff. It bums me out.
jokes.setup: Phoebe feeling left out of adventures
jokes.punchline: Casual description of pregnancy as just 'pregnant stuff'
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's casual dismissal of the miracle of pregnancy is perfectly in character

jokes.text: What the hell was that? / Probably some European goodbye thing he picked up in London. / That's not European. / It felt French.
jokes.setup: Confusion about Joey's kiss goodbye
jokes.punchline: Misidentifying it as European, then the 'felt French' observation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with the specificity of 'felt French' adding absurd expertise

jokes.text: It came in the mail today. It's 72 long-stemmed, red roses. One for each day I've known and loved Emily... cut up into mulch.
jokes.setup: Delivery of Ross's romantic gesture
jokes.punchline: Emily destroyed them and sent them back as mulch
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark payoff to Ross's romantic gesture - the mathematical precision makes the destruction funnier

jokes.text: Monica's going to make potpourri.
jokes.setup: Ross's romantic roses destroyed and turned to mulch
jokes.punchline: Monica's practical plan to make potpourri from the ruins
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica efficiency - turning romantic devastation into home improvement project

jokes.text: It's not raining. / I can't catch a break!
jokes.setup: Ross wanting to wander dramatically in the rain
jokes.punchline: His frustration that even the weather won't cooperate with his misery
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Ross's self-pity - even his romantic suffering is thwarted

jokes.text: I dropped some socks.
jokes.setup: Urgent interruption of conversation
jokes.punchline: Trivial excuse about dropping socks
jokes.explanation: Weak excuse for obvious eavesdropping, but appropriately clumsy for the situation

jokes.text: You did not drop any socks!
jokes.setup: Rachel's transparent excuse about socks
jokes.punchline: Monica's frustrated calling out of the obvious lie
jokes.explanation: Monica's directness and control-freak nature calling out Rachel's deception

jokes.text: Well, you're fired. / You can't fire me. I make your decisions, and I say I'm not fired! Ha!
jokes.setup: Rachel trying to fire Monica from decision-making role
jokes.punchline: Monica using her decision-making power to prevent being fired
jokes.explanation: Brilliant logical paradox perfectly suited to Monica's controlling nature - very quotable

jokes.text: I'll kick that door in if you give me some sugar.
jokes.setup: Joey offering to help with locked door
jokes.punchline: His condition requiring a kiss for the favor
jokes.explanation: Joey's opportunistic use of the kissing situation for personal gain

jokes.text: Rachel wants to take swing dance lessons. Which I think is a really stupid idea. It's dangerous. She won't get what she wants. And who knows who she might end up hurting?
jokes.setup: Monica trying to talk Rachel out of telling Ross about her feelings
jokes.punchline: Using swing dancing as transparent metaphor while Ross listens
jokes.explanation: Clever use of metaphor that's transparent to audience but not Ross, good writing technique

jokes.text: Monica's right. Swing dancing can be tricky.
jokes.setup: Monica's swing dancing metaphor about Rachel's feelings
jokes.punchline: Ross's oblivious agreement about actual swing dancing
jokes.explanation: Ross's complete obliviousness to the subtext makes the metaphor work

jokes.text: My God! Think of the massacre.
jokes.setup: Ross canceling teddy bears he sent to Emily
jokes.punchline: Describing five teddy bears as potential 'massacre'
jokes.explanation: Ross's tendency to overdramatize extends to imagining teddy bear violence

jokes.text: Well, we thought we would all go to a picnic... in Central Park! / Central Park? / All of us. All day. / That sucks!
jokes.setup: Building up a special trip for Phoebe
jokes.punchline: Revealing it's just Central Park and Phoebe's blunt reaction
jokes.explanation: Good buildup and deflation, Phoebe's honest reaction is appropriately blunt

jokes.text: That's not a trip! I just came from there. What'll we high-five about at the stupid park?
jokes.setup: Phoebe's disappointment about Central Park 'trip'
jokes.punchline: Her practical objections including the high-five concern
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's specific complaint about high-fiving opportunities is oddly logical

jokes.text: Hold on a sec there, Mr. Kissy.
jokes.setup: Joey about to leave with his new kissing habit
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sarcastic nickname for Joey
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler sarcasm with appropriate nickname for Joey's behavior

jokes.text: Just bringing some culture to the group. / That's fine. Just don't bring it in my mouth. It makes me want to puke.
jokes.setup: Joey defending his kissing as cultural
jokes.punchline: Chandler's graphic objection to unwanted mouth contact
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Chandler's visceral reaction and specific phrasing

jokes.text: Atlantic City! Oh, that's a great plan! Whose plan was that? / Mine! / Wait. It was my plan. / No... I said we needed a new plan. And I came up with Atlantic City. Which is the new plan.
jokes.setup: Phoebe asking who came up with Atlantic City plan
jokes.punchline: Multiple people claiming credit with increasingly complex logic
jokes.explanation: Standard escalation of credit-claiming, slightly overwritten but functional

jokes.text: I'm going to go pack my ass off.
jokes.setup: Enthusiasm about Atlantic City trip
jokes.punchline: Slightly vulgar twist on common expression
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay that's more crude than clever, below average quality

jokes.text: Paper. / Yeah? What's it about? / The events from around the globe.
jokes.setup: Question about what Ross is reading
jokes.punchline: Ross's overly formal description of newspaper content
jokes.explanation: Ross's pretentious way of describing a newspaper fits his academic character

jokes.text: Once at work... I thought carbon dating is fossilized... I'm really trying to tell you something here.
jokes.setup: Rachel trying to make a point about people being wrong
jokes.punchline: Getting lost in scientific details before catching herself
jokes.explanation: Rachel's tendency to get sidetracked and her realization of it is character-appropriate

jokes.text: What? Was that a joke? Because it's mean.
jokes.setup: Ross not immediately responding to love confession
jokes.punchline: Rachel's confused and hurt reaction to perceived cruelty
jokes.explanation: Rachel's vulnerability and confusion create genuine awkward tension

jokes.text: Because I just heard it! I heard it! And it's ridiculous! I mean... You're married! You're married, and it's just ridiculous!
jokes.setup: Rachel explaining why she's laughing at her own confession
jokes.punchline: Her repetitive, nervous breakdown of the situation's absurdity
jokes.explanation: Rachel's nervous laughter and self-awareness create realistic awkwardness

jokes.text: When I said it, I sort of floated up out of my body. And then I heard myself say it, and then the floating Rachel... I was like, 'You are such an idiot!'
jokes.setup: Rachel describing her out-of-body experience during confession
jokes.punchline: The floating Rachel judging the real Rachel
jokes.explanation: Creative visualization of embarrassment with good specificity about 'floating Rachel'

jokes.text: 'Hey, Ross! Where's the missus?' 'Don't know!'
jokes.setup: Ross realizing the absurdity of his situation
jokes.punchline: Mock conversation highlighting his ignorance of wife's whereabouts
jokes.explanation: Ross's self-awareness about his ridiculous marital situation is refreshingly honest

jokes.text: That's what I said!
jokes.setup: Ross saying it's always great when someone tells you they love you
jokes.punchline: Rachel's vindicated reaction referencing her earlier argument
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Rachel's earlier reasoning, provides satisfying validation

jokes.text: Let's roll some bones. Hey, Joey! High-five for rolling bones.
jokes.setup: Enthusiasm for Atlantic City gambling
jokes.punchline: Awkward attempt to make 'rolling bones' worthy of celebration
jokes.explanation: Forced enthusiasm and awkward high-five request, functional but not inspired

jokes.text: Well, don't worry. I call shotgun.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's water breaking and need to rush to hospital
jokes.punchline: Joey's immediate concern with seating arrangements
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey selfishness in crisis moment with excellent timing and economy

jokes.text: Honey! High-five! The babies are coming!
jokes.setup: Emergency situation with Phoebe going into labor
jokes.punchline: Treating birth as high-five worthy moment
jokes.explanation: Continues high-five running gag but feels forced in the emotional moment

jokes.text: It's too bad they didn't see us having sex.
jokes.setup: Joey kissing Phoebe and Rachel goodbye
jokes.punchline: Chandler's inappropriate suggestion about what else Joey should do publicly
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler inappropriate timing and sexual humor, fits character well

jokes.text: Do you know anything about women?
jokes.setup: Chandler's crude comment about public sex
jokes.punchline: Monica's rhetorical question highlighting his cluelessness
jokes.explanation: Standard Monica exasperation with Chandler's inappropriate behavior, functional closer
